sample_comments.title: Cool and fun and strange location. Stuck in 1998 like a time capsule
sample_comments.positives: Cool location big building with fun and strange activities
sample_comments.negatives: Dirty pool, no towels available in pool area at 5pm, outdoor gym was a joke, --- more importantly Aircon didn't work in the room all that well. Thankfully staff brought us a fan but still.

sample_comments.title: Disappointed
sample_comments.text:
Room service was below average. 
Dead bugs everywhere for my son to chew , would explain why he didn't eat at the restaurant. Overall cleanliness was poor

sample_comments.title: staff were friendly but was very dissapointed didnt get room i originally booked
sample_comments.positives:
staff at embers was great all though they mixed our meals up with another table 
cafe was quick with food and cocktails 
was nice having king towels in bathroom.
sample_comments.negatives:
booking,com double booked our room! had to give us a smaller room without a balcony 
they were meant to leave a bottle of wine in our room never received it even after contacting reception.
there were no coffee cups left in room for us to have coffee in morning.
pillows to soft old pillows

sample_comments.title: Dont bother with this, rather book oher quaint cottages for cheaper and more character
sample_comments.text:
The Good: Location and the nice view you. Breakfast spread was great with lots of choices and delicious. 
The Meh: Service was hit and miss - the porter was helpful and polite but other staff were indifferent and cold and very rude on the phone. Reception insisted on checking in at 3pm when we arrived at about 230pm.
The Horrible: Long strands of hair found on bed when we checked in. Bathroom water has weak pressure. Another long hair found on towel while drying myself. Yucks! I really wonder if they bother to clean the room. Very disappointing cleanliness for a five star resort. No complimentary water. Really awful since we just came from a fabuous five star hotel from Sydney. Dont bother with this hotel which seems to be cruising by only on the brand name and its location. I suggest booking any other accomodation here with more character and for a cheaper price. Not value for money at all.
sample_comments.response:
Dear Travis
Thank you for your review and feebdack, we appreciate hearing from all of our guests.
I would like to apologise to you sincerely for a failed stay with us, certainly far from our want or intention at any time and very disappointing to read. We pride ourselves on our high level of warm and personalised service and I am so sorry this did not shine through during your stay. Your comments have been reviewed with our Front Office Manager as well as our House Keeping Manager to ensure their teams are made aware.
Thank you for sharing your concerns once again,
Kind regards
Scott O'Neile
General Manager

sample_comments.title: Not good than before
sample_comments.positives: Enviroment is good, and staff is nice.
sample_comments.negatives: It supposed to be a kids-friendly place, but the swimming pool did not have any kids area; kids' trains are not available on Monday/ Weekdays; Food is not good, buffle breakfast has very less options. Facilities are super old. I came here 1 or 2 years ago, where the kids will get a duck toy, but not this time. I feel a little bit disappointed.

sample_comments.title: Disappointing
sample_comments.text:
Arriving after 4 pm and there was no porter insight to help with the luggage. We have to struggle oursleves with the luggages . How could this happen to a 5-star hotel?

After we got the key and go to our non-smoking room, we smelt cigarette smokes covered by aerosol spray. We called the front desk and was told someone will come and take a look but nobody came. I have to go to front desk to get a change of room.

While in the room, we were disappointed to feel that the attention to details are just not there:
1. The faucet has a lever that is longer than the spout, it will poke your face when you try to wash your mouth.
2. A rack is placed very close to the basin that your elbow constantly hit on it. 
3. The shower curtain is too short that soap water gets splashed out all over the floor. 
4. The shower head handle is worn out that there are sharp edges protruding that will cut your hand. 
5. One of the pillowcases looks like it has been used before. It is crumpled with dirt inside.

sample_comments.title: It was a nice place - but expected better for the price we've paid.
sample_comments.positives: The resort is secluded, away from the main town.
sample_comments.negatives: Not enough quiet places. Arcades are old. Hard to navigate around the resort with a lack of signage, especially from car parks further away.

sample_comments.title: Oldish
sample_comments.text: Alright but too expensive for the quality of the infrastructure
sample_comments.positives: Personnel, huge, view

sample_comments.title: I will most likely choose somewhere else next time we come to the mountains.
sample_comments.positives: The location of the Fairmont is fabulous and the views are great.
sample_comments.negatives:
The 2 days we were there was overrun with children. They were in the bar area, in the expensive dining room and running around the hotel. It was overwhelming and made our stay very disappointing. It's not that the children were there, but that they were noisy and running around, and the parents did nothing to keep them quiet and resect the other guests. For the amount we paid ($353 per night for each room) our stay was spoiled with noisy children.
In addition, our breakfast wait staff not only started clearing dirty plates while I was eating, but she also reached between us to retrieve dirty cups and plates - and not just once! Basic manners at all times please!

sample_comments.title: Overcrowded and costly
sample_comments.text: Getting overcrowded now and expensive for a room for what it is. Been a long time since an upgrade

sample_comments.title: It's okay- expensive for what it is
sample_comments.positives: There's no food available anywhere at night and it is not close enough to go into town. Staff who checked us in was 'not happy to be there'- soured our first impression. Signs were up saying places were inaccessible due to events but events looked to be long over? We were unsure if we could access and did not want to bother front desk again. Pillows were cheap.

sample_comments.title: More Appropruate For Family With Younger children.
sample_comments.text: Hotel a bit run down. Breakfast variety can be better. Thanks.

sample_comments.title: Acceptable
sample_comments.negatives: Quality of rooms. Loose shelves. Not clean (toenails on floor!)

sample_comments.title: Good property, staff could be a bit more friendly and accomodative
sample_comments.text:
The property is huge and has many facilities like indoor pool, jacuzzi, gaming arena etc. However most other facilities were closed such as the boating in duck pond etc which would be open only in school holidays. 

The staff at the reception were to the book and refused to acknowledge my accor plus booking as I had booked through Agoda and wouldn't even offer the welcome drink or even upgrade me to a better room in spite of availability also refused late checkout requests. 

We had paid a premium to buy breakfast and there were fruits, cornflakes, bread etc. But no hot breakfast options for vegetarians. 

There were no restaurants other than dominos who would deliver to the hotel. 

Property is good and big but ensure the facilities are open in case you are looking for a particular facility.
sample_comments.response:
Dear Arun,

Thank you for sharing your feedback. We're happy to hear that you enjoyed the property and its many facilities, including the indoor pool, jacuzzi, and gaming arena. We apologize for the closure of some activities, such as the boating at the duck pond, and appreciate your understanding that certain facilities are seasonal.

We're truly sorry that your experience with our staff didn't meet your expectations. We are committed to providing exceptional service, and your comments will be addressed with the team to ensure we are more welcoming and accommodating in the future.

Regarding the Accor Plus member benefits, please note that these are applicable only when booking directly with the resort or through the Accor website. As for late check-out requests, these are subject to availability, and unfortunately, we were unable to accommodate your request this time.

We also regret that the breakfast offerings did not fully meet your dietary needs. We will review the selection to p

sample_comments.title: Acceptable
sample_comments.positives: Clean, access to trails.
sample_comments.negatives: Captives, away from restaurants and the town. If you don't have a car, good like finding uber or cabs.

sample_comments.title: Above average
sample_comments.text:
Check in was delay till 4pm. So many ppl were waiting for check in and the only coffee shop/lounge were full as it was small with limited seats. 

The play rooms/area are dirty and smells with food and garbage (not one day, but a few days we experience the same.) The outdoor adventure area, pony riding are all closed. Nothing as described on the website. Disappointed.

sample_comments.title: The facilities/ kid's activities are the only saving grace. The rooms require significant refurb.
sample_comments.positives: The facilities and activities for the children is fantastic. The day spa was also amazing.
sample_comments.negatives:
For that price point the hotel was not comfortable and I would expect more: 
- there was very little water pressure in the showers which made it hard to shower and rinse my hair properly. The Facilities team tried changing the showerhead, but it made no difference. We ended up having to shower the kids down at the pool area where the water pressure was much better. We then needed to walk through reception and the hotel to get back to our room.
- the glass screen between the bath and the toilet was unable to be secured so there was a significant gap as it moved which left a lot of water all over the floor and an extremely soggy bathmat.
- the bed was hard and uncomfortable 
- the on-site restaurants booked out a week before our stay, so we travelled into town for food. The room service offerings are pretty average and not much variety. The menu fit all on one page.

sample_comments.title: Comfortable but needs improvement
sample_comments.text:
Room interior and lightings out-dated. Lights provided were too dim at night. 
Bathtub sealing not working at all thus unable to fill-up the tub :(
Not within walking distance to the nearest mall.

sample_comments.title: Comfortable and Clean, but nothing spectacularly "Fairmont" about it.
sample_comments.positives: Beds very comfy. Room was clean.
sample_comments.negatives: The food at Sublime Lounge was not good. Rock-hard pot pies, mouldy lettuce in Caesar Salad.

sample_comments.title: Mediocre
sample_comments.text:
This place was very average. Front desk staff were great and very helpful. Most on floor staff had great customer service skills. Kind and friendly.
Booked seafood buffet in one of their restaurants downstairs. No grilled fish, calamari. The hot food was cold. 
Spa was not in service(the reason i booked this hotel) and had a grime build up anyway.
Management, although kind and calm lacked any initiative to problem solve- it was like dealing with someone who was sleeping.
The place needs an upgrade. Its age is showing.

sample_comments.title: Above average
sample_comments.positives: Definitely a family friendly hotel. Hotel rooms were very large. We were in a King Suite. The activities on offer for the kids were great, although not all were of interest to my daughter. There was one staff member at the lobby bar who made the most fantastic cocktails!!! And the lovely maintenance/gardener gave my car loving 2yo and I a quick trip on the ride on quad bike which was SO kind of him! The train ride was wonderful, we went twice!
sample_comments.negatives: Lack of cleaning/disinfecting, especially on the kids playgrounds and arcade rooms. The hand sanitisers were generally always empty and hard to find. The rooms were old, but that was expected and not as bad as I thought. The facilities and grounds were definitely tired. The pool towels were always out of stock when we tried to go to the pool.

sample_comments.title: Above average
sample_comments.text: Quite expensive for a simple overnight stay. Felt in need of a makeover. Carpark some distance from the entrance so would suggest offloading luggage at the entrance then parking. Nice location.

sample_comments.title: Our experience definitely fell flat of our expectations - has potential to be great.
sample_comments.positives:
Stayed for 3 nights with my 5yo. The location was fantastic. Short drive from all the blue mountain hot spots. I highly recommend the short drive to Sublime point lookout. It was breathtaking! Also scenic world is an absolute must. Blackheath markets were worth the short drive (even saw snow!) 

We were fully obsessed with the rainbow slide!!! Pricey- but worth it. (Only open on one day we were there out of 4) LOVED the ice skating as well! Again, very expensive for 90 min ($65) but we had a blast. Could've been user error on my part- but I thought the amenities like ice skating, movies, slide, train, arcade etc would be included in the price of the hotel. But you need to pay for almost everything (mirror maze and playground is free) the indoor playground is great for younger kids! 

Room service was great. Chicken Caesar salad was super tasty, as was the kids cheeseburger. And the fruit plate exceeded expectations! 

We loved the little shop front - expensive- but loved being able to get our sugar hit from the pick n mix lollies! Also great to buy gloves/hats as it was colder than expected!

Concierge was extremely helpful every time we needed them! Front desk were great too- you could tell it was v busy so I'm sure some things were missed. They were always soo friendly and smiley. overall a good experience checking in and out with front desk/concierge. I read a few reviews complaining about parking. We never had an issue and went during a long weekend. You can drop off your bags to concierge when checking out/in then walk to get your car. Not that dramatic. And the parking is free (thank god lol)

 I truly think this property has SUCH potential if they do a bit of a revamp, clean a bit more, and maybe lower the prices of the activities onsite. Wish I had more to say that was better- but a lot just fell flat.
sample_comments.negatives:
We checked into our room around 4pm and it was FREEZING. I'm talking -2 at night. I went to turn up the heater and it only goes to 26. We had to have a portable heater in our room the entire duration of the stay and it just didn't heat properly.

Also, not sure if they put us in a pet friendly room (I'm very allergic to animals) but my asthma was out of control every time we spent longer than 20 min in the room. Very run down room as well - cracked sink, peeling wallpaper, dingy bath. Just wasn't up to standard of how much we paid.

Beware- the walls are PAPER THIN! We could hear EVERYTHING. Our last night there - there was a party that went on til 3am. Loud music, shouting, banging - it was awful (on a Sunday night) You can literally hear a pin drop in the hallway.

The breakfast was a standard buffet breakfast- lots of options- however I found the "hot" breakfast items to be stone cold. Great that it's open til 1030am.

I'll say again- that the amenities they have showcased online were pricey. $2 per slide on the rainbow slide (both the train and merry go round weren't operating the days we were there Fri-Mon) arcade games are a rip off- no chance in winning anything out of those machines - and $2 per game. Pretty standard price for arcade games but you don't win tickets or anything so it's just the entrainment. The mirror maze was awful - smelled horrible in there like vomit! The whole games area actually had such a strong awful smell. The indoor pool was extremely dirty - and a couple degrees colder than I would have liked during winter. You turn into an icy pole as soon as you get out. The spa was closed for maintenance whilst we were there - so can't comment on that.

Overall- I had really high expectations of Fairmont as the pictures and reviews looked sooo promising. And I do believe they have an opportunity to be fantastic - but just didn't quite hit the mark for us.

sample_comments.title: Hit and Miss - will not return
sample_comments.text:
Stayed there for 2 nights with my 2 young children. At first impression it was a nice hotel with great facilities and welcoming staff. However by the second day I found the hotel to be lacking in service and quality amongst other things.

I had ordered room service the second evening and shortly after ordering the hotel experienced a complete blackout (while my son was in the shower). We walked upstairs as the lifts and phones were not functional and stayed in the car to charge my phone and watch some movies as both my children were quite scared of being in the pitch black room (my daughter is only 20 months). While we were waiting the hotel lights came back on after approximately an hour so we started to return back to our room. Once we made it down the stairs to our floor we were greeted by the room service attendant who immediately asked upon seeing us what our room number was. When I told him he responded in a very rude and accusatory tone that he had been knocking on our door and waiting for us. It was as if we had inconvenienced him by leaving the premises and rather than apologising for the blackout he had the audacity to say we had made him wait!

When we returned to our room the power was still not working (ceiling lights were ok) so there was no tv or charging stations or internet to use. When I contacted reception they merely said that it was a rare occurrence, it was a local issue and not the fault of the hotel.

The following morning I received a note under the door from the hotel apologising for the inconvenience the blackout caused and that it was a local issue - the letter was seemingly left to ensure the guests were aware that it was not the caused by the hotel.

My son went to brush his teeth only to find there was no running water. I called reception and the phone rang out (this also happened the previous day when I ordered room service). I called again and after a long wait someone finally answered. I advised them we had no running water and they

sample_comments.title: Above average
sample_comments.positives: The school activities, the view and outdoor area.
sample_comments.negatives:
The room. Very old amenities, not cleaned properly. 
Old dusty carpet, old not fully functional shower head and tap. The rooms were not vacuumed, cleaned or tidied every day. 
Breakfast was decent.

sample_comments.title: Tired hotel that could be much better
sample_comments.text: We really looked forward to staying at this 'Fairmont' hotel having stayed at other hotels in this brand. We were quite disappointed. While the weather wasn't great and that is certainly not the fault of the hotel, we had hoped we would be staying at a top quality establishment. Sadly this was not the case. The hotel was opened nearly 30 years ago and seems to have been devoid of maintenance in the intervening years. The bathroom was small and dark with very poor lighting but there was sufficient to see the mould on the tiles above the bath. The TV was susceptible to cutting out because the cabling to it had poor connections. Indeed we had to call their maintenance man out who didn't really seem surprised by this fact. The woodwork around our room window frame and the exterior woodwork in the public area had so much paint peeling that it was mostly bare and starting to rot. The carpets in the room were stained in several places. The doors on the elevator were faulty to the extent that when our baggage trolley was exiting they partially stuck for a few minutes. Service in the hallways was not much better. When we went to breakfast, the person in a nearby room had placed their room service trays outside for collection. They were still there in late afternoon - about 6 hours later. There main restaurant was closed in the evening leaving only their coffee shop eating area open. Not what you expect for a high end hotel which charges top dollar. We ended up going into the local village (and having a great dinner) but the hotel should have had a better on-site dining option. In the hallway near the swimming pool there was clearly several leaks in the ceiling as they had placed buckets and towels on the floor to catch the drips of water. There's so much more wrong with this hotel but I probably don't need to provide any further examples.

sample_comments.title: Expensive without extras you would expect
sample_comments.positives: Great big room
sample_comments.negatives:
Quilt on bed far too hot. Why is this done when so easy to use blankets or a light quilt 

Also no biscuits with yea and coffee. An expensive stay with limited consideration for comfort

sample_comments.title: not value for money
sample_comments.text: The room and water kettle are not clean. The shower is not of good water pressure.
sample_comments.positives: nice environment

sample_comments.title: Good facility for families but not everything available mid week in school holidays
sample_comments.positives: The breakfast was good
sample_comments.negatives: The room was sooooo noisy from inconsiderate people upstairs :( I called multiple times with little help to the situation. Also school holiday activities were finished before the last 2 weeks of holidays! Didn't make sense at all with so many families there and seeing the activities but not being able to use them on non weekends.

sample_comments.title: restaurant staff service was extremely poor.
sample_comments.positives: size of room, location, and garden

sample_comments.title: Above average
sample_comments.positives: Great location in the heart of Blue mountains
sample_comments.negatives: Outdated furniture, former glory of a once great hotel. Canteen food, nothing other than tea in the room, not even a bottle of water.

sample_comments.title: Service with a smile.. but no service!
sample_comments.text:
This hotel has so much potential... but just hasn't got there yet. Arriving at 3.30pm we were informed that our room wasn't ready yet... so a free drink on the house and an hour later we were still waiting. After chasing the bar staff for an update we went down to our room on level two. Located just near the pool the room was a good size and the bed comfortable, however the pillows are awful. The bathroom is small and the shower terrible pressure wise, and housekeeping could probably spend a little longer in there. On our second day we returned to the room to find the mini bar attendant reading our newspaper from the couch area, not sure who was more surprised... We were very confused with the restaurant and bar opening hours, the coffee lounge didnt open, and the Eucalypt restaurant was open outside of normal advertised hours - very confusing and we only realised when trying to leave to patron another restaurant in leura. Staff are friendly but so disorganised, the bar area is a nightmare, and there appears to be no cohesion between staff. Breakfast is a disaster, extremely busy, not enough staff and impossible to get new cutlery etc when using a table previously occupied. We tried the pizza from the bar on the second night, it was undercooked and pretty average, and the trio of dips somehow got downgraded to a duo...?
The hotel is being renovated, but it all seems rather slap dash. The exteriors need a good sweep and the cobwebs inside and out are not appealing.
sample_comments.positives: Location

sample_comments.title: Great fun for small children.
sample_comments.positives: Great activities for little kids. Beautiful location.
sample_comments.negatives: Very dated interior. The blockout curtains had holes in them. The kids play area had a terrible stale smell.

sample_comments.title: Noise. No for light sleeper
sample_comments.text:
Too noisy.

Woke up multiple times due to the noise from corridor and from window (during early hours when kid is playing on the playground).

Not complaint about the people who cause the noise. Their 'noise' is not unreasonable

sample_comments.title: Great kids activities onsite...but stay elsewhere.
sample_comments.positives: The kids' activities were varied and entertaining. We especially liked that ice-skating was onsite.
sample_comments.negatives: The hotel is in desperate need of a renovation. It is clearly a family-focused hotel, but the rooms look corporate, and the carpets are threadbare (not really conducive to kids on the floor). The bathroom was missing most of the grout and we even had a tile come away from the wall during the stay.

sample_comments.title: Above average
sample_comments.text: Rooms dated, most staff are friendly

sample_comments.title: Above average
sample_comments.positives: great location helpful staff
sample_comments.negatives: poor room sound isolation ,we could easily hear our next door room smaller moves the facility is quite old and the amount of money you pay does not match the service you receive

sample_comments.title: Dated and unclean. Please renovate!
sample_comments.text:
Been coming here since the 90's. Used to be great but unfortunately needs a good renovation in the rooms and amenities, to bring back the Fairmont luxury and magic!
Carpets need replacing and restaurants revamped. Nostalgia is the only thing going for it at the moment.
Would love the see it come back with a fresh reopening.

sample_comments.title: It was good location peaceful but we have been put in the ground floor where you cna hear early and nights all babies na
sample_comments.positives: Location

sample_comments.title: Can do better than this
sample_comments.text:
It's our 3rd time returning with my wife and kid as we were recommending good facilities & atmosphere for family and kids.
But this time, we had a bad experience. 1st of all, water in both swimming pool & whirlpool is obviously dirtier than last 2 visits. We always came during school holidays, there shouldn't be any excuses for maintenance of all facilities, specially the water. My boy 5-year-old boy felt belly pain and threw up twice on the night of 15/7, and caught high fever starting around 12pm. As it got worse, I walked to Reception myself around 1pm for first-aid assist, and they explained no Panadol and opened the Kiosk shop also found no any related medicine for under 7-year-old, also cooling patch is not available. Then I had to drive all the way out to 24hrs petrol store for Panadol after 1am midnight. I meant as family-choice hotel, specially during school holidays, it's not hard for the management to predict there are a lot of kids inhouse, those common medicines should be available in case of any emergency use. Front Office and Manger on Duty should be very clear abt this and provide assist right away.
Indoor swimming pool toilet & swimming /whirlpool should be paid much more attention as it's absolutely the hottest spot during winter holidays. Towels were running out from 16/7 5:20pm not replenished more than 45 mins, a lot of guests were complaining abt that.
Moreover, room floor corridor was quite dirty.
Other than these, the new Jumping castle was told 2 tickets for 10mins at the Concierge expanding desk while checked in (15/7), however, it became 5mins only when played (honestly, it's quite bad in value of money). 
Lastly, staffs are generally still friendly and nice, movies are well organized, Magic Craig is still doing a good show, kids like him.

sample_comments.title: Enjoyable golf group getaway.
sample_comments.positives: Staff were excellent and helpful. Dinner at Embers was very good. Rooms were comfortable. The hotel is a little dated.
sample_comments.negatives: The golf course and club house facilities were below the standard we were expecting.

sample_comments.title: Best for families, not couples.
sample_comments.text: Great for families, bad for a quiet retreat. Very kid friendly and expect lots of yelling, screaming, and running about with overwhelmed staff unable to do anything about it. Food was great for all meals eaten on site, but again paying big bucks for dinner while kids running in the dining room. Property a bit over work and under maintained in areas. Staff was great but absolutely overwhelmed and exhausted. Rooms okay, but needing some attention. Surprised to find the Fairmont brand associated with this property since Fairmont has always stood for excellence.
sample_comments.response:
Dear Joan,

Thank you for sharing your feedback with us. We sincerely apologize for not meeting your expectations during your stay at Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains.

We understand that our property is more family-oriented, and we truly appreciate your comments regarding the lively atmosphere, which can sometimes be less conducive to a peaceful retreat. We will certainly review our approach to maintaining a quieter environment in public areas for those seeking a more serene experience.

Regarding your experience with the property, we take your concerns about maintenance very seriously. We are constantly working to improve the resort and will be addressing the areas you've mentioned. We also appreciate your kind words about our staff, and we recognize the challenges they face during peak times.

We value your input, and we are committed to ensuring that Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains continues to uphold its reputation for excellence. We hope to welcome you back in the future and provide
sample_comments.room_type: Deluxe King Room with Spa Bath

sample_comments.title: Acceptable.
sample_comments.positives: The early check-in was very efficient and easy and the room was comfortable.
sample_comments.negatives: I booked for dinner on Sunday night for 7.00pm. in the Fairmont's restaurant We arrived at approx 6.55pm and were shown to our table. We were immediately provided with water but then had to wait, UNSUCCESSFULLY for in excess of 20 minutes for any further attention from the wait staff, despite making eye contact with the waiter who had shown us to our table. Also, it appeared that tables that were seated after us were giving their drink/food orders while we bided our time. This was totally unacceptable to US, so we left. It certainly coloured our opinion of The Fairmont.

sample_comments.title: 1 star for cleanliness in 5star facilities
sample_comments.text:
I read many review saying the room is dirty, so I'm a bit worry. Prior to check in I request for extra cleaning since my family have allergies.
However is so hard to get the confirmation if my request can be done. I tried email and call few times to follow up and only got reply on my check in day saying they will extra dust the room. However when I arrived, there is food stain and crumbs in the carpet, both bedside table are so dusty, in the bathtub, towel and bathroom floor have hair. No slipper and do not disturb sign in the room. The table was so wobbly and is a hazard. The shower pressure was low (I already knew from the review that I read prior), but the hand wash water pressure is high and splash everywhere. The breakfast was exactly the same in 2 consecutive day. Usually 4/5 star hotel have 1/2 item to rotate.
However, to be fair, the kids facilities was good and that the only reason we stay there. 
Is such a shame a nice resort with good facilities lack of the room cleanliness, which is essential and basic standard in hotel.
sample_comments.response:
Dear Nelson,

Thank you for sharing your detailed feedback with us. We deeply regret that your stay did not meet the high standards we strive for, particularly in terms of cleanliness, and we truly apologize for the issues you encountered during your visit.

We understand the importance of maintaining a clean environment, especially for families with allergies, and it's concerning to hear that the room was not prepared to your expectations. We sincerely apologize for the food stains, dust, and hair you found in the room, as well as the other maintenance issues like the wobbly table and low shower pressure. This certainly does not reflect the quality we aim to deliver, and we will address these concerns with both our housekeeping and maintenance teams to ensure they are rectified.

Regarding the breakfast, we understand your disappointment with the lack of variety, and we appreciate your feedback. We strive to offer a rotating menu to cater to all tastes, and we will review this to ensu

sample_comments.title: Great location, fun for kids but certainly needs a renovation!
sample_comments.positives: Lovely location and so many kids activities.
sample_comments.negatives: Pool was cold and couldn't be enjoyed. Gym wasn't clean, peloton bike didn't connect, rooms need update and more detailed cleans.

sample_comments.title: Living off its Name
sample_comments.text: A nice building but very pricey and not worth the money
sample_comments.response:
Dear Peter,

We very much appreciate your comments regarding your stay at the resort.

It was very pleasing to see there were aspects of your stay that were enjoyable. I am sorry however that some areas did not meet your expectations. 

Thank you again for sharing your experience, and we hope to welcome you back to the resort again very soon.

Kind Regards,

Charlie Young
General Manager

sample_comments.title: Overall ... OK. Hotel is positioned for families ... please renovate worn timber / carpets.
sample_comments.positives: Overall experience
sample_comments.negatives:
Rooms and hotel foyer look "tired" e.g. scuffed timber work around entry; mis-shaped and worn carpets in Room 102.

Children riding their bikes around the foyer and corridors.

sample_comments.title: Disappointing
sample_comments.text: Resort was at capacity and very busy with young families. Our rooms were located on the second floor just off the lobby next to Kidzone and pool - extremely noisy with kids and parents consistently yelling and running down the corridor. Rooms were tired and housekeeping service was only available on request - this wasn't advised at check-in and we only discovered it when we returned to our rooms in the afternoon to find notes under our doors. We were really excited for a weekend away at this resort but were disappointed with our experience.

sample_comments.title: Lovely resort, good location among the Blue Mountains
sample_comments.positives: Location was ideal for us. The views when you walk into the lobby was stunning.
sample_comments.negatives: The whiskey bar opening hours were limited. The Sublime Lounge menu options were limited along with room service, ideally for a facility of the size there would be a better selection. The resort had a lovely relaxed feel but could do with some TLC but wouldn't stop us from staying again.

sample_comments.title: Indifferent
sample_comments.text:
I usually stay here every few months with my daughter and am usually very pleased with the hotel. I was dissapointed this time there were no tissues in the hotel or bottle opener in room. I was told I would need to call every time I wanted a bottle open as they only had one opener in the whole hotel, bottles needing opening should have not been in the mini bar. The bed sheet was ripped. 
The concierge and desk staff as usual were very helpful.
sample_comments.response:
Dear Fran
Firstly thank you for your ongoing loyalty to the Fairmont Resort & Spa - it is very much valued and appreciated by us all here.
I am pleased our concierge and front desk reception staff were very helpful throughout your stay.
I was extremely disappointed to read of your experience with both the bed linen and the bottle opener. This is, as you would know from previous experience, highly unlike our usual product and service levels. I wanted to assure you your comments have been discussed with our House Keeping manager for immediate review with her team as it is not acceptable. Thank you so much for taking the time to bring this to our attention, we certainly appreciate the opportunity to immediately rectify these issues.
Fran, we look forward to continuing to welcome you back in the future and are confident your normal service levels will be experienced once again.

Kind regards

Scott O'Neile
General Manager

sample_comments.title: Enjoyable - never been to the Blue Mountains before
sample_comments.positives: Able to stay in the same room after our conference for our private booking so no need to move belongings to a different room
sample_comments.negatives: Golf Club lunch was very disappointing - staff didnt seem interested about ant feedback

sample_comments.title: Old and dated room
sample_comments.text:
Peaceful nice setting but out of the way,
Only one restaurant open for dinner during the week , hot spa not open either , disappointed . Swimming pools would be nice in summer. If your not paying golf then mabe look for a different location that has more restaurants and things to do.

sample_comments.title: Overall a good stay.
sample_comments.positives: Great location and facilities
sample_comments.negatives: Staff seemed like they were forced into the job and not enjoying it one bit, except for the Arab concierge guy and Indonesian lady at the restaurant.

sample_comments.title: Not worth it
sample_comments.text: Pricey, poor breakfast spread, extras like Wifi connection and bottled water were chargeable at exorbitant rates.

sample_comments.title: 60th Birthday Surprise
sample_comments.negatives: Embers restaurant. Seated in the Jamison section. Cold, clinical and noisy. I requested a table of 7 in Embers but was told they don't do this. Upon arrival saw a large group seated there. Wouldn't book there again.

sample_comments.title: good hotel good location
sample_comments.text: It was the second time we family spent 2 nights there. It is quite and tidy. Near the Leura town and Three Sisters Atrractions
sample_comments.positives: location, environment, quite

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Great variety with lots to do
sample_comments.negatives: The property looks very dated and tired. Hard to justify the price considering the condition of the property

sample_comments.title: Generally comfortable.
sample_comments.text: Generally Good but Hotel still lacking 85 some areas. No assistance with bags, long corridors to carry bags to lifts. Amenities like toiletries & fresh milk or free water not available in rooms. Standard rooms very basic & still needing upgrade. Hotel needs more general amenities for guests. Piano or music in bar, food at bar & shops etc
sample_comments.positives: Nice location. Scenic views. Close to shops

sample_comments.title: Average
sample_comments.positives: Restaurant and view
sample_comments.negatives:
The experience was okay overall, but I don't think it's worth coming here just for the holiday activities. The ticket prices have doubled in the past six months, which makes it feel less affordable.

Jumping Castle: This was a bit disappointing. It's $6 for 5 minutes of play, and they time it with a stopwatch. It's also quite high and steep, which scared my kid after just one try. It would've been nice if they mentioned the time limit when selling the tickets.

Rainbow Slide: Much better. At $2 per slide, it's still fun and relatively reasonable, even though it's pricier than before. My kid really enjoyed this one.

Bathrooms: Not very convenient for kids. There's no handheld showerhead, and you have to step into a bathtub to shower, which makes it tricky for little ones.

Overall, while there were some enjoyable parts, the price increases and lack of family-friendly features make it less appealing for a holiday outing.

sample_comments.title: Great Resort
sample_comments.text:
Hotel room is fair, but clean & tidy.

Location is a bit far away from city centre and difficult to find nearby resturants.
sample_comments.positives: Quiet & Peace

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Pet friendly room
sample_comments.negatives: Lack of noise insulation at our front door. We frequently heard noise at all hours of the night and early morning which disrupted our sleep and our dog

sample_comments.title: Not recommended for couples
sample_comments.text: More than a resort & spa I would describe this place as a summer camp for families. The place is designed for children's entertainment and although the facilities are nice they are dirty and unorganised. Staff seemed to be overwhelmed with the excess of guests and young kids running wildly all over the place so it was hard to find tables in the restaurant or someone to clean the tables once you found one. I had to clear and wipe the table my self as after asking at least 3 staff no one went there to help, even the guy who brought our food to the table left the dirty dishes from the previous guest there. The buffet breakfast felt like being in a 5 years old birthday where no parents were looking after the kids. The pool area was the Wild West of children, and smell like urine to say the least. The rooms had thin walls so you could hear the fights between children's and the parents arguing when the voices raised and seems that on holidays no one had an early bedtime. Very disappointed about this place. Only recommended for parents o people who love kids and noise.
sample_comments.response:
Dear Carolina,

Thank you for providing us with your feedback. We are truly sorry to hear that your experience at Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains did not meet your expectations, and we appreciate you taking the time to share your concerns.

We recognize that our resort is designed with families in mind, which can sometimes create a lively atmosphere that may not be ideal for those seeking a quiet, relaxing retreat. We apologize if the noise levels impacted your experience, and we will certainly review our approach to ensuring a more peaceful environment for all guests.

We are also concerned by your comments regarding the cleanliness and organization of our facilities, as well as your experience in the restaurant. Please know that this is not the level of service we strive to provide. We will address these issues with our team to improve both the dining experience and the upkeep of the property.

Your feedback is invaluable, and we will use it to ensure we continue to improve our offeri

sample_comments.title: Has definitely moved to market itself as avenue for families.
sample_comments.positives: Beautiful location have stayed many times over the years.
sample_comments.negatives: Bathroom was tired, shower head very average.

sample_comments.title: Old hotel
sample_comments.text: This hotel caters more to local tourists and guests who plays golf. It is not ideal for guests who wants to explore the blue mountains. There are no available guided tours from the hotel and no sufficient information for those who wants to explore the outdoors. It is a old hotel.

sample_comments.title: Love the facilities but not our dinner
sample_comments.positives:
Breakfast was great, my kids really enjoyed the Buffet selections. Kids facilities was excellent, keep them occupied.
Location is not far from Leura and Katoomba.
sample_comments.negatives:
The lobby lounge only open until 8, staffs are little slow. One person was wearing sunglasses at night?!
Taste of our pizza and calamari were not good. The lounge should have opened until late

sample_comments.title: michael
sample_comments.text:
The hotel appears a bit 'tired', with reduced maintenance levels.
Restaurants were below standard with primitive wine lists.
The noisy conferance participants removed any illusion of a first class hotel.
Carpark was completely ful, mainly occupied by visitors to the golf club.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: facility was beautiful, buffet breakfast was good
sample_comments.negatives: we came on an anniversary weekend hoping to use the facilities, however there were so many kids we couldn't use the pool,spa or sauna at all the whole time we were there. perhaps a separate adults spa and sauna would be ideal.

sample_comments.title: Should have kept the room service tray
sample_comments.text: Shocked I was charged $4 for the room service tray, who on earth charges $4 for a tray that was returned, I should have kept it!

sample_comments.title: Comfortable, good location but very noisy!
sample_comments.positives: Location was amazing with a beautiful view, friendly staff and comfortable bed. I purposely booked this hotel as it had a bath.
sample_comments.negatives: A lot of children, older kids knocking on bedroom doors at 10pm at night. Screaming kids also during the day, no quiet area at all. I wouldn't rate it a 4 star hotel, rooms were comfortable but average. Apart from location, not worth the price for me. Most likely suitable for families but not a solo traveler (teacher) during school holidays!!

sample_comments.title: Good Location
sample_comments.text: I booked this hotel on early 2015 and stayed for one night. That was really great. Clean and nice room. But this time the toilet flushing button broken. The shower tab pull off. Hotel staff needed to call the maintenance guy to fix them. Some facilities are old and broken.
sample_comments.positives: Kid friendly, Great Location and Staff friendly

sample_comments.title: Beautiful resort - but avoid weekend stays if you're a childless couple looking to unwind
sample_comments.positives:
The view (particularly by the Ember Lounge Fireplace) was really beautiful, and had all the right amenities for a great stay, including a pool, sauna, library, billiards room, kids zone and more. The King Bedroom was spacious. If you are a family with children then it has lots of activities to keep your little ones busy. 

Close to town so plenty of great restaurants and bars to visit, and near a lot of the Blue Mountains attractions.
sample_comments.negatives:
While I couldn't fault the hotel itself, if you are a couple without children, I would highly recommend you avoid weekend stays. My husband as been twice for mid week work trips and he had a very calm and pleasant experience. However, when we visited for a relaxing weekend away, we both left even more stressed than when we arrived. This was because the entire time we were surrounded by screaming children - at breakfast, at the bar, in the pool and sauna, and even in our room we could hear children running up and down the hallway yelling and banging on doors at 7am. This is quite a family friendly hotel with lots for the little ones to do - including a kids zone(we did not realise this at the time of booking), so if you have kids this place would be great for you!

Additionally I would recommend that you book things well in advance. We could not get a restaurant booking at Embers or No.1 Sublime Pt, or a spa booking when trying to book on the day we arrived. They did offer us a reservation at the bistro at the golf club - only available on the Friday as Saturday was booked (we checked it out but it wasn't the vibe we were looking for for a weekend away so we left, however would be find for those who are happy with a casual bistro environment).

sample_comments.title: nice and quite
sample_comments.text: happy
sample_comments.positives: comfy, quite and family oriented

sample_comments.title: Can't go past for kids
sample_comments.positives: I liked all the activities for kids and in room dining.
sample_comments.negatives: There was a note left for previous/future guest, bathroom had hair left on floor that wasn't ours - didn't seem like it had the time required for full clean...

sample_comments.title: comfortable
sample_comments.text: We would have felt more welcomed if the staffs were a bit more friendly. didn't even get an acknowledging smile from them. Very indifferent attitude. Smiles welcome guests.
sample_comments.positives: the hotel design, large comofrtable rooms and beds

sample_comments.title: Good
sample_comments.positives: Location
sample_comments.negatives: Got charged for restaurant service we didn't order

sample_comments.title: Comfortable stay in large hotel
sample_comments.text:
We had an ok stay here - the room and hotel is comfortable but it is looking a bit tired and could do with a refurb. The bathroom in particular was dated, and not very clean either unfortunately. I thought it was overpriced for what it offered. 

This would be a great hotel for a conference or a wedding, but it's a bit out of the way of things if you're a tourist without your own transport.
sample_comments.positives: Quiet location

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives:
Kids zone
Grounds 
Train ride for kids
sample_comments.negatives: Dated and in need of a paint and good clean

sample_comments.title: Midweek getaway
sample_comments.text: Our stay was comfortable but the room lacked a few basic amenities such as a mini bar and snacks a shower cap in the bathroom

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Resort offers a good range of recreational options - especially for families. The rooms are a good size with plenty of room to move around and the decor is comfortable inside the rooms - they are also nice and clean with comfortable beds; the rooms are dark (amazing block out curtains) and the heating made the whole resort nice and snug.
sample_comments.negatives:
Main gripe I would say is the customer service was only fair. The staff are pleasant enough but have little knowledge of what is going on outside of their 'box'. One example was my experience prior to arrival of arranging pre payment (due to members of my group were arriving before me) and allocating names to rooms under my booking was unsatisfactory - I spoke to 3 different people, had 3 different conversations, had 3 confirmations of details received and all okay and my group still had a problem accessing their rooms as told no details on the system? 

The food choices are very few and could be markedly improved - more options where families can sit and have a normal cafe meal would be recommended - burgers, schnitzels, toast in the morning - all of these are simple and popular foods that families enjoy. Also on this - waiting 25 minutes for a cup of coffee is ridiculous! it wasn't even busy. Everyone seems to be moving around on the upper level with lots of space downstairs saved for the higher end food (which not all can afford of course) it just doesn't make sense so in wrap up the food is expensive with limited choice (we picked up takeaway 2 out of the 3 nights we stayed as was underwhelmed by the meal we had at the resort the first night). 

In relation to decor - the common areas show some wear and tear, would suggest a bit of polish on the floors to make them shine and the lift areas would benefit from some TLC.

sample_comments.title: An awesome stay at Fairmont Resort
sample_comments.text: We stayed at Fairmont Resort for couple of days, and we had a great time. It is a very big place, great amenities, perfect for families they have a Timezone style gaming rokm for kida to enjoy, 2 swimming pool, they also have a sauna and spa. And a health wellness centre, and 6 different Restaurant, they also provide free breakfast, which is really great lots of food to enjoy, and staff are all very friendly. I would recommend it. Parking fee is $15 per night, but if you have *** Membership which is free to join, youcan get the free parking.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: This hotel is very family friendly, lots of things for kids to do. As parent, it is most important thing I don't need to do too much to keep my 7 years old girl busy. All the staff are very nice and helpful.
sample_comments.negatives: This hotel is just very old, need big renovation. Even beddings are very old. Because it is so old, it doesn't feel clean enough for me.

sample_comments.title: Average hotel and outdated.
sample_comments.text: Very average hotel.. outdated.. age is showing up, toilet is poor, showers are not working well.

sample_comments.title: Great location but needs a refurb in the spa/pool area
sample_comments.positives:
The beds are really comfortable, staff really friendly and location is excellent.
 Breakfast is also great
sample_comments.negatives: The pool was really dirty unfortunately and it's very much a family hotel which is confusing when it's also a golf resort and conference space. It's quite run down

sample_comments.title: Great potential but average service and facilities
sample_comments.text: So much potential for this property and could use refurbishment and renovation to make it modern and valuable. The beds were comfortable but the room assigned was noisy and closer to the entrance road (room 307) and loading bay and spoiled the sleep (and too late to request a change) plus no water bottles were provided and upon calling, was asked to drink tap water and ended up in a trip to Coles and alter found, there were vending machines available to get water. So, value for money is moderate and front desk service lacked genuine warmth and customer service. Overall, not satisfactory though the stay is just one night.

sample_comments.title: Amazing views
sample_comments.positives:
The views are absolutely stunning, it also has a big territory that allows you to feel much closer to the beauty of Blue Mountains. 
The resort also has a wide variety of facilities that are worth exploring, swimming pools, spa, heaps of kids entertainment. Generally a very good place to stay.
sample_comments.negatives:
Two things stood out as genuinely frustrating during our stay.

First, the service speed was a real issue throughout. I waited roughly 45 minutes for a single coffee, watching one staff member walk back to the fridge to collect milk individually for each cup in the queue. At breakfast the following morning, four sandwiches and four coffees took close to an hour to arrive. One of the sandwiches was also brought out incorrectly and had to be replaced. That one was for a child, so we had to manage a small crisis at the table while waiting. For a resort of this standard, the pace and accuracy of service fell well short of expectations.

Second, the parking situation was a real letdown. I received a message from the resort encouraging me to pre-book parking to avoid public fees, which I did. But when we went to exit, the barrier required payment of a public fee regardless. We ended up paying twice. Given that the system uses plate recognition technology, this should have been a seamless, automated experience. Instead it felt like the pre-booking offer was misleading, whether intentionally or not.

sample_comments.title: Pleasant stay
sample_comments.text: Very dated hotel, certain areas stunk. But the rooms were comfy and plenty of activities for the kids

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Facilities are excellent for family trip. My kids have experienced ice skating for the first time in their life, there are also swimming pool, playground and giant slide inside the hotel. It's about 5mins drive to a fantastic lookout nearby.

sample_comments.title: Never again
sample_comments.text: This resort is a playground for kids & almost everything you have to pay extra. Booked with breakfast included but absence upon checking so we pay again. The aircons is too warm despite a 20c setting render you have to open the windows. Staff are nice though, mostly
sample_comments.response:
Dear Lawrence,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We sincerely regret that your stay at Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains did not meet your expectations.

We apologize for the inconvenience regarding the breakfast, and we understand how frustrating it can be when such details are missed upon check-in. We will review our processes to ensure clearer communication moving forward.

Regarding the air conditioning, we regret that the temperature was not to your satisfaction. We will have our maintenance team investigate the issue to ensure it is functioning properly for future guests.

We are pleased to hear that you found our staff to be welcoming, and we truly appreciate your kind words. Your comments are valuable to us, and we will continue working to improve the overall guest experience.

We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back in the future and provide a more enjoyable stay.

Warm regards, 
Guest Relations Team

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Sauna
sample_comments.negatives: Stores closed too early

sample_comments.title: two night with a 3 and 10 years old
sample_comments.text: The facility used to be magnificent, but it has become a lot more run down. I have put the clean up sign since 11 am, but nobody has come in to clean until 3 : 30 pm when I asked them to come to clean. And when the cleaning lady came, she said she has no vacuum cleaning with her, and do I want just towel or do I need the full service. It is quite disappointing. The pony service was discontinued without any notice. There was not a lot to play that is working. I think the location is nice but overall experience is disappointing.
sample_comments.response:
Dear Ai,

Thank you for sharing your feedback. We sincerely apologize for the issues you experienced during your stay at Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains.

We are very sorry to hear that the cleanliness of your room was not up to standard, and we understand how frustrating it must have been to request service multiple times. sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Great facilities and location
sample_comments.negatives: Not enough food options around

sample_comments.title: Improvement needed.
sample_comments.text:
The good: lots to keep kids occupied, especially in the holidays. Their cinema plays a movie every 2 hours, indoor pool great in winter, the magic craig show was great and was free, lots of other activities too. Our room was cleaned when we went for breakfast. 
The negatives: couldn't book dinner in their restaurant for the whole 3 nights because they were booked out. The only restaurant available without booking didn't have an extensive menu. Breakfast was the same everyday, very crowded and canned beans and tomatoes were used rather than fresh ones. Seen staff wiping tables and letting scraps fall onto the floor. Resort floor in many areas wasn't clean. Most kids activities cost money, which should be free because room cost is double in the school holidays. They were also all booked out. Was only able to book one activity for the three days, and buy tickets for their large outdoor slide.
sample_comments.response:
Dear Fatme,

Thank you for sharing your feedback with us. We're glad to hear that your children enjoyed the various activities, including the cinema, indoor pool, and the Magic Craig show---it's always great to know that our guests appreciate the entertainment options we provide.

However, we are truly sorry to hear about the challenges you faced during your stay. We understand your frustration with the difficulty in booking dinner at our restaurant, and we apologize for the inconvenience this caused. We will look into better ways to manage reservations to avoid such issues in the future. Additionally, we're sorry that the available dining options didn't meet your expectations, and we appreciate your feedback on the menu variety.

We also regret that the breakfast experience and cleanliness of some areas did not meet the standards you expected. Your comments regarding canned ingredients, crowded spaces, and staff procedures are being taken seriously, and we'll address these with our team

sample_comments.title: Happy that i came
sample_comments.positives: So beautiful. The receptionist was amazing
sample_comments.negatives: Heard babies crying a little from the next room

sample_comments.title: Not my cuppa tea!! Probably good for family with kids
sample_comments.text: Location is okay, near 3 Sisters look out, the town its located in is 'Leura' its kinda boring and super quiet. Hotel is probably great for family(Family Weekend Getaway). There are plenty stuff/somewhat activities that kids these day like to do. Parents booked the room here, dump the kids free ranging, go off to do whatever they want then parents off to the hotel's bar and get shitfaced. This is how I see it. My own opinion tho :)

sample_comments.title: Perfect for a short winter holiday
sample_comments.positives:
I got very good customer support before arriving. The kids loved it.

It would make it even better if there was a kids club I can leave my kids to do an activity and just relax
sample_comments.negatives: That the games for the kids were very centered in video games. Also, i would have liked to be informed that I had to make a reservation to have dinner at the main restaurant.

sample_comments.title: Fairmont Resort Australia NSW
sample_comments.text:
It was the same menu for breakfast at least variety the following day will be better.
Bathroom had no bath soap the container was empty and no large bath towels.

The corridor was dirty from day 1 to day three.Cleaners would attend to the rooms but forget to vacuum and tidy up the corridor.

sample_comments.title: Lovely rooms but had to go out to eat / disappointing
sample_comments.positives: Clean and comfy
sample_comments.negatives:
When we arrived it took almost 25mins to check into . The manager ( who checked us in) was slow and non communicative and unfriendly . 
The Restaurant - no bookings available on the first night we arrived in the main restaurant or the golf club . We felt hotel guests should have been advised to book ahead .
Not enough staff to cater for guests for Breakfast the following morning - took almost 10 mins of queuing to check in for breakfast . Had to go back and que at check in to order a coffee . 
The whole restaurant/ breakfast part seemed totally disorganised and chaotic .
Food average - not restaurant standard .

sample_comments.title: Good for young kids
sample_comments.text: We have been before about 4 years ago, not much has been changed or updated. Good for kids, staff friendly and place clean. Food not great and over priced. However there are plenty of places to eat in Leura.

sample_comments.title: Beautiful location!
sample_comments.positives: The location is beautiful. Good access to hiking trails. Great views of the Blue Mountains.
sample_comments.negatives:
The property is old. It does need updating. The beds were just okay. Pillows were terrible. Haven't been replaced in quite a while.
Seems like it's a property geared toward families.

sample_comments.title: Excellent Location But Some Letdowns
sample_comments.text:
The setting is very nicely done and the place looks great when you enter. Surroundings are also quite nice with scenery and layout as well. The only letdowns were:

1 - The spa wasn't working and there was no disclosure until we asked the next day.
2 - The on site restaurant was slow, expensive and service was poor

We asked for more bread and were told they ran out and had to ration it for other customers.

After ordering the grilled asparagus side we were told they ran out, however lo and behold our chicken dish came out with asparagus.

Our food arrived well after other tables sat down and others that arrived before us got theirs after us as well.

3 waiters were not attentive - and the place was hardly busy - there were maybe 6 tables altogether.

The food was not to our liking either - attempted fancy dishes tasted funny.

The best part of the stay was the Whiskey bar which was amazing - I am not sure if the two business are affiliated in any way I would guess they are not.

All in all - the stay was simply not worth the $300 per night that they charge - recommend to find something with more value elsewhere.

sample_comments.title: Ok
sample_comments.positives: Lovely view
sample_comments.negatives:
Shower over bath difficult and allowed water on floor. 
Breakfast was always cold!

sample_comments.title: Friendly staff
sample_comments.text: This hotel is more suited for families with young children because of the facility. Don't come during school holidays you won't be able to relax, kids were everywhere during our stay. We came to relax & have sometime for ourselves but it was too noisy, kids were running outside our room's hallways, they're also in the swimming pool all the time, you can see/hear them screaming eg in the restaurant. It wasn't a pleasant stay at all. We also couldn't contact the front desk from our room, & the concierge & other areas of the hotel to book the restaurant, order food or stuff from housekeeping nobody answered their phones. We ended up calling the front desk from our private phone using their outside number. The carpets were outdated a bit untidy. Breakfast menu was the same every day. The only positive thing was the staff friendliness & they tried to offer a solution with our issues. I'd like to especially mention Angeline & Sam from the front desk who were helpful about our issues, David Oh from the restaurant who was accommodating & made our dining a pleasant experience. I'll recommend this hotel for families with young children but not for couples.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: I love how there was lost of facilities available for children but I feel it needs a bit of update

sample_comments.title: The hotel was lovely and the staff were ok but i found the food to be a hit or miss deal
sample_comments.text: One night the meal i ordered was fantastic and whilst i ordered the exact same meal the following night it was obviously another chef's interpretation of the meal which left me bewildered and disappointed as it wasn't what i had received the night before

Johnathan, we hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back again in future where we are confident you will receive our high level of both product and service that has so many of guests returning time and again,
Kind regards
Scott O'Neile
General Manager

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: The staff was amazing and very helpful

Adam at concierge is great and attentive. 
Receptionists are not helpful at all during check in and even asking for concierge services. They should assist to call when Adam is busy. 

Kids facilities are great. But should patron often by staff in case guests need help. 

Location not convenient unless by bus epxlorer or drive only.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Location, room, food, kids play areas.
sample_comments.negatives: Expected indoor pool and spa to be warmer.

sample_comments.title: Worth of money

sample_comments.response:

General Manager

sample_comments.title: Fantastic Family Friendly Hotel
sample_comments.positives: We absolutely loved the place. It has lots of activities for our daughter. We enjoyed our stay. We will surely come back for a longer stay.
sample_comments.negatives: Limited food options.

sample_comments.title: Luxury needs to come with service
sample_comments.text: The resort is great, good location, and all the bells and whistles. The service was a bit laxed - some staff looked happy to serve and provided 5* service, while others seemed intent on making life difficult. Perhaps it was low season, but after experiencing many 5* resorts throughout Asia, this was disappointing. If you can ignore the staff/service, the rest is great. I think it's normally a lot more expensive here, so I'm not sure it's value for money.
sample_comments.response:
Thank you for your review.
We are pleased you enjoyed the resort, our idyllic location and the facilities in general.
I was most upset to read of the service levels you receive. This is such a main area of focus for the resort, with much training, resources and time allocated to it. As a result we usually receive accolades for the warmth and personalised nature of our service and I am so sorry this was not apparent during your visit. I wanted to assure you this is not reflective of our normal levels and we thank you for brining it to our attention.
We do hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back in future where we are confident you will experience our highly efficient and warm service throughout all elements of your stay.
Warm regards
Scott O'Neile
General Manager

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Everything you need on this hotel is here most specially for family with the kids this is a great place to stay. There food in Restaurant is exceptional .
sample_comments.negatives: The only problem is the noise with the kids early in the morning just next to our room i hope they can separate the rooms with the family and kids so people can relax more.

sample_comments.title: Not as good as Expected
sample_comments.positives: 1) Location was fine

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: the resort itself is great for families
sample_comments.negatives: breakfast buffet needs ALOT of improvement, the reception lounge/bar/cafe area needs more staff, over a 45 minute wait for coffee only and the bottom fire place area should be opened for lunch as well, such a beautiful space wasted in the day!

sample_comments.title: Comfortable stay in king bedroom
sample_comments.text: We just stayed one night. Though we didnt utilise many of hotel facilities , we had a great time in this hotel. it's located near Leura mall, it'd be best place if you like to play golf.
sample_comments.positives: The big size of bed, spacious room, great view from the entrance

sample_comments.title: Enjoyed our stay and will visit again
sample_comments.positives: Very relaxed atmosphere. Lots of facilities for kids
sample_comments.negatives: Eating facilities limited.

sample_comments.title: Nice hotel but some areas still need a refurb!
sample_comments.text:
the hotel is set in a beautiful location and the newly refurbished rooms are very comfortable and spacious. the outdoor pool is very nice although the indoor pool, squash courts and gym are in need of an upgrade (and if you go to the hotel midweek the pool is taken over my kids swimming classes for most of the afternoon/ early evening). the hotel restaurant and bar was ok but very over-priced.
the staff are all very friendly and we had a lovely stay.
sample_comments.positives: Beautiful surroundings, good sized rooms

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives:
The location was beautiful. The indoor pool; jacuzzi and sauna perfect fun. The resort has good facilities but a little outdated . 
The staff at the reception was really good and gave us a warm welcome.
Thank you for the wine as we were celebrating my mum's 60th birthday.
sample_comments.negatives:
The breakfast could have had more option. Like simple things eggs to order. 
When we reached for breakfast we were informed that after 45 mins the table needs to be emptied as they are busy. Now that is not something I appreciate. There were so many restaurants in that property and they can be opened too if there are a lot of guests.
When we asked for room slippers they said that there's a shortage and they can't give us 2 slippers. It's a 5 star common you cannot give such reasons.

sample_comments.title: Not too bad
sample_comments.text: Need to have a car otherwise quite a walk to go to Leura shopping area.
sample_comments.response:

We hope you enjoyed your visit overall and look forward to welcoming you again.

Guest Relations Team
Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains

sample_comments.title: Very good

sample_comments.negatives:
Ambiance of Italian restaurant. Shame it looks so basic. With some tweaks it could be a more inviting space.
Rooms. No complimentary water or mini bar which was surprising.

sample_comments.title: A bag of chips opened by hotel staff before check out
sample_comments.text: Packed everything and went out for Breakfast, a bag of chips was picked from the bag, opened and moved to another desk. Luckily I moved valuables to the car. Bad reception, cause I mentioned this to the team and the receptionist said she will investigate and let me know what happened. And was trying to call back after check out and no answer, no response, not anything.

sample_comments.title: Good Food. Great staff. Just need a bit of maintenance works.
sample_comments.positives: Firstly, I would like to commend you on the overall quality of our stay. The service was impeccable, and the food was delightful. The efforts put into maintaining a high standard of hospitality were evident and much appreciated.
sample_comments.negatives: However, I couldn't help but notice certain aspects of the resort that could benefit from attention. Specifically, the physical condition of the complex seems to need remedial works.

sample_comments.title: Poor rooms, great grounds
sample_comments.text: Consistent with other reviews, the rooms are in poor condition. The bed was very soft & reception confirmed the use the same mattress throughout. The bathroom was a step up from a public change room. If you have low expectations & get a good price it's still worthwhile if you're going with kids as there's lots to do within the grounds. The staff are wonderful
sample_comments.response:
Dear Melanie,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback about your recent stay. We apologize if the condition of some of the amenities provided did not meet your expectations, and we are sorry to hear that the mattress was not to your liking. Bedding preferences can vary widely, and we strive to accommodate all our valued guests' needs.

Our team is dedicated to creating a welcoming atmosphere, and we are pleased to hear that you found our grounds enjoyable and our staff to be exceptional.
Your insights are invaluable as we continuously seek to improve our accommodations and overall guest experience. We are actively reviewing the feedback like yours to ensure we can continuously enhance the comfort and quality for our valued guests.

Thank you once again for your comments. We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back in the future for a more enjoyable stay. 

Kind regards,
Guest Relations Team
Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains - MGallery Collection

sample_comments.title: Great place to stay but slightly pricey during holidays
sample_comments.positives:
1.I like it because it has so many activities for kids during this school holiday. 
2.The activities staff were so friendly and kind to kids. 
3. The view was just beyond stunning
4. The ice rink gave us another memorable experience
sample_comments.negatives:
1. We have hand soap in our shower room above the bath tub. I checked there wasn't any body shower gel at all in my bathroom. You asked us to use hand soap to wash? I don't know if that's normal? 
2. We stayed two nights, but the room hasn't been clean at all after the first night. We didn't request not having housekeeping. 
3. It's a school holiday time, there is only two staff at the reception. We waited for 20mins just want to check out. And when we were checking out, the staff told me the bank machine wasn't working so asked me to leave my back card details for which I did leave the B bank card details. However, 2hours later, I received the deduction from my A bank card which I paid for the checked in. Luckily I was still in the hotel, so I can go to reception to retrieve my bank card details. Why is that?

sample_comments.title: Blue mountain
sample_comments.text: The hotel is quite old and you can feel it in the rooms and amenities. However, the staff are wonderful, they made our friend's bday really special. They are also very kind and helpful.
sample_comments.response:
Dear Happy,

Thank you for sharing your feedback. We're delighted to hear that our team made your friend's birthday special and provided you with friendly and helpful service. We appreciate your honesty regarding the condition of the rooms and amenities, and we are continually working to improve our facilities. Your comments are valuable, and we hope to welcome you back soon for an even better experience.

Kind Regards, 
Guest Relations Team 
Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains

sample_comments.title: It's a pleasant family hotel but unfortunately suffers from poor service considering it's 4 star rating.
sample_comments.text:
Bad check in experience, we understand 3pm check in, yes we did arrive at 2.35pm. Told room is guaranteed to ready at 3pm, Concierge will call at 3pm. No call, join the queue at Concierge after 3pm to be told room not ready, talk to reception. Different person at reception apologise but unfortunately not time frame when room will be ready. We explain we have a seven year old daughter who wants to get changed and head to the pool. Unfortunately still unable to give us a time. My wife created a bit of a fuss, shortly after, a room was ready. While we're in the room changing Concierge call and say our room is now available!!!!
Next night in the Sublime Lounge we order three dishes for dinner, two dishes arrive, unfortunately one missed. Caesar Salad missing, young guy serving says wasn't on the docket. Asked him to check, after quite awhile he returned confirming it was on the docket. Dish arrived well after our family had finished our dinner.
Finally as you are aware the Smoke Alarm issue in the middle of the night of 13 January. Extremely loud, terrifying our young daughter and other children in our part of the hotel. Crying children echoing down the corridor in the middle of the night isn't pleasant.
sample_comments.response:
Dear Clifford,

Thank you for taking the time to share your detailed feedback following your recent stay at the Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains - MGallery Collection. We value your input and appreciate your honest comments.

We sincerely apologize for the issues you experienced during your stay, particularly with the check-in process, the delayed Caesar Salad, and the disruption caused by the fire alarm in the middle of the night. We understand how these incidents impacted your overall experience and the comfort of your family, especially your young daughter.

Your feedback regarding the check-in process has been shared with our front desk team to ensure better communication and efficiency in the future. We are also addressing the delay in your dinner service with our restaurant team to prevent similar occurrences.

The fire alarm incident is regrettable, and we are taking measures to minimize such disruptions in the future while ensuring the safety of our guests.

We appreciate your un
